key_insight: Atomic Cluster Expansion (ACE) potentials can bring screw-dislocation predictions in bcc refractory metals much closer to DFT, but only when the training database and element-specific energetics are carefully chosen.
engineering_relevance: This enables more reliable atomistic design and lifetime prediction for high-temperature refractory alloys by improving simulations of dislocation glide, Peierls barriers, and kink-pair nucleation.
method: Extended an existing DFT database to train a set of ACE interatomic potentials for group 5 and 6 refractory metals.
Evaluated screw-dislocation properties including Peierls barriers, glide planes, and kink-pair activation enthalpies.
Compared ACE predictions against DFT and checked consistency with Kocks' law and a line-tension model for selected metals.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports near-DFT accuracy for Mo and W, substantial improvement for V, Nb, and Ta, and reliable kink-pair activation enthalpies for Nb, Mo, and W.
watch_item: A single-humped Peierls barrier is not sufficient to validate dislocation glide behavior, so transferability must be checked against multiple dislocation-specific metrics.
paper_title: Atomic Cluster Expansion Potentials for Screw Dislocations in BCC Refractory Metals
